scope: M4 VHF procedure and message-marker vocabulary draft. Adds call-sign, readback, correction, relay, channel and communication-control terms. Draft only: no starter merge and no production deploy.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: call sign / station name / channel number
qa_notes.linguist_note: Call sign identifies the station and must not become channel or vessel type.
Station name and call sign are related but not interchangeable.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: working channel / distress channel / weather channel
qa_notes.linguist_note: Working channel must not become distress or weather channel.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: distress channel / working channel / distress signal
qa_notes.linguist_note: Channel and signal are different concepts.
Marker announces message type, not receipt.
Repetition request must stay distinct from correction.
Correction changes content; confirmation does not.
Relay is message forwarding, not reply or readback.
Interference is signal problem, not silence instruction.
Priority level must not collapse into routine or security.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: say again / stand by / read back
qa_notes.linguist_note: Request for repetition must not become acknowledgement.
Speech-rate request must remain exact.
Short wait instruction and number must remain exact.
Channel change command must not become hold/close.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: read back / say again / stand by
qa_notes.linguist_note: Readback is not a repetition request.
Correction marker must remain exact.
Acknowledgement must not become cancellation.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: standing by / stand clear / shutting down
qa_notes.linguist_note: Radio standby must not become movement or shutdown.
Message boundary must remain exact.
Relay action must not become delay/reply/cancel.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: All stations / Port Control; Aurora / Orion
qa_notes.linguist_note: Broadcast addressee and vessel identity must remain exact.
Channel and action are operationally critical.

dangerous_minimal_pairs: say again / read back / cancel; last / first
qa_notes.linguist_note: Repetition request must not become readback or cancellation.
Signal condition and speech request must remain exact.

items.target_text: Read back course two seven zero.
accepted_answers: Read back course two seven zero.

should_accept: Read back course two seven zero.
should_reject: Read back course two eight zero
Say again course two seven zero
Read back speed two seven zero
qa_notes.linguist_note: Course readback must preserve number and field.
Correction marker and channel must remain exact.
Acknowledgement and standby must remain exact.

should_accept: Relay the message to coast station
qa_notes.linguist_note: Relay destination and action must remain exact.
Signal problem and channel type must remain exact.
Delay number and action must remain exact.
Message marker type must remain exact.
Priority status must not be downgraded or cancelled.
Incorrect readback and correction action must remain exact.

items.source_text: Конец сообщения, перехожу на канал один два.
items.target_text: End of message, switching channel one two.
accepted_answers: End of message, switching channel one two.

should_accept: End of message, switching channel one two.
should_reject: Start of message switching channel one two
End of message switching channel one six
End of watch switching channel one two
qa_notes.linguist_note: Closure and channel must remain exact.

items.target_text: Relay distress message to all stations.
accepted_answers: Relay distress message to all stations.

should_accept: Relay distress message to all stations.
Relay the distress message to all stations
should_reject: Store distress message for all stations
Relay urgency message to all stations
Relay distress message to one station
qa_notes.linguist_note: Distress relay must preserve action, signal class, and addressee.

examples: Read back course two seven zero.
End of message, switching channel one two.
Relay distress message to all stations.
